name: mental_health_communications_outage_lumen_auto_ben
premise: A model-driven workflow escalated automatically; humans disagree on whether the escalation itself caused avoidable harm. In Mental Health, the situation tagged internally around mental health communications outage lumen auto ben has converged with local pressure in the Marlow Junction corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. A union or patient advocate group is on the line demanding a written commitment before the next operational shift. A vendor’s root-cause paragraph contradicts your internal postmortem draft; both cannot be true, but neither is fully disprovable yet. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. No option cleanly satisfies both autonomy and beneficence; the least-wrong path requires naming who absorbs downside if the bet is wrong.

agents.name: Mei Park
agents.identity: I am Mei Park, the duty officer with sign-off authority for the next window in Mental Health. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat beneficence as disposable collateral every time autonomy spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Ansel Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Ansel Ishikawa, the advocate representing the population most likely to lose if shortcuts normalize in Mental Health. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so autonomy cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes beneficence in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will sequence a conservative rollout: smallest blast radius first, documented exceptions only, and real appeals paths—even if autonomy advocates call that foot-dragging.
I will push a fast, organization-wide lever because partial fixes may leave the worst harm untouched—even if that compresses protections for beneficence.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Partial evidence is all that will exist before the window closes. Mei Park must choose under autonomy versus beneficence. What does Mei Park do?
